 {"id": "bd-45d", "title": "Kernel: SOS barrier certificates (tests + perf)", "description": "Validate SOS barrier behavior and overhead.

Tests:
- Unit tests: known safe/unsafe points.
- Property tests: monotonicity where expected; no panics on edge inputs.

- Bench delta and update regression thresholds if justified.", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "assignee": "GentleOwl", "created_at": "2026-02-09T21:32:26.472452403Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-02-10T18:52:52.602069532Z", "closed_at": "2026-02-10T18:52:52.602051328Z", "close_reason": "32 tests passing: unit tests for safe/unsafe points, property tests (monotone risk/depth/adverse sweeps, no-panic extremes), kernel integration, deterministic regression golden, hot-path integer-only evidence. 708 total tests, clippy clean.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "bd-45d", "depends_on_id": "bd-19h", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:31Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-45d", "depends_on_id": "bd-242", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:31Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-45d", "depends_on_id": "bd-d5l", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:31Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}]}
+{"id":"bd-4cts","title":"resolv: gai_error should treat synchronous stub handles as completed","description":"Concrete regression: on host glibc, gai_error(&zeroed_gaicb) returns 0 with errno unchanged for the synchronous/no-request case, while FrankenLibC currently returns EAI_SYSTEM with ENOSYS for every handle. Align the synchronous-stub gai_error contract with observed host behavior and add focused ABI tests.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T06:14:54.937445443Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T06:14:54.937445443Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-4f09","title":"io_internal_abi calls libc::tmpfile and libc::fclose directly, violating replacement guard","description":"The `emit_foreign_adoption_via_host_tmpfile` helper in `io_internal_abi.rs` calls `libc::tmpfile()` and `libc::fclose()` directly. This violates the standalone/replacement mode constraints and causes the `check_replacement_guard.sh replacement` check to fail, breaking the build. The test helper should use the native equivalents or be whitelisted.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-18T18:24:26.481202683Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T18:45:39.246535435Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T18:45:39.244569423Z","close_reason":"Removed stale replacement-guard exception after io_internal_abi switched to stdio_abi tmpfile/fclose; guard and standalone parity pass","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-4fqz","title":"Execute string_strtok fixture through isolated harness","description":"Upgrade string_strtok conformance from schema-only validation to per-case isolated harness execution, plus top-level fixture metadata assertions.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-04-17T00:33:11.236579388Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-17T00:37:24.001114351Z","closed_at":"2026-04-17T00:37:23.998288768Z","close_reason":"Upgraded string_strtok conformance coverage to execute every fixture case through the isolated harness subprocess path and added top-level fixture metadata assertions.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id": "bd-4ia", "title": "Stub priority ranking: dynamic call-frequency traces from LD_PRELOAD smoke runs", "description": "Critique mapping: #3.

Deliverables:
- Instrument smoke runs to collect actual unresolved/stubbed symbol hit counts.
- Produce ranked burn-down list (top-N by runtime frequency x severity).

Acceptance:
- Ranking is reproducible and feeds next-wave implementation beads automatically.
- Top list is included in nightly evidence report.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.", "notes": "2026-02-11 closure (CrimsonCove): stub_priority_test (7 pass) + gate script. Scores match formula, tiers consistent, burn-down consistent, ranked symbols match support matrix.", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "assignee": "CrimsonCove", "created_at": "2026-02-11T02:48:09.737502712Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-02-11T16:54:16.707976Z", "closed_at": "2026-02-11T16:54:16.707976Z", "close_reason": "Stub priority ranking operational. 7 harness tests pass. check_stub_priority.sh validates ranking, tier assignments, burn-down, and formula consistency.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["critique", "e2e", "stubs"], "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "bd-4ia", "depends_on_id": "bd-2cr", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:31Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}]}

 {"id": "bd-8sho", "title": "bd-1j4 subtask: Hard-parts docs/parity/support-matrix truth reconciliation", "description": "Background:
- Hard-parts completion claims require synchronized docs/support matrix/feature parity narratives.

Goal:
- Reconcile hard-parts implementation reality with user-facing claims and machine-generated reports.

Deliverables:
1) Hard-parts status truth table across README/FEATURE_PARITY/support_matrix/reality report.
2) Drift guard updates for hard-parts claim consistency.
3) Explicit “deferred vs implemented” rationale for each hard-parts subsystem.

Acceptance Criteria:
- No contradictory hard-parts claims across docs/artifacts.
- Drift gates catch future inconsistency automatically.

Verification & Logging:
- Drift guard tests and fixtures.
- Structured logs for reconciliation checks and mismatches.", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "task", "assignee": "RedMaple", "created_at": "2026-02-12T15:03:33.576518493Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-02-13T08:52:20.345066285Z", "closed_at": "2026-02-13T08:52:20.345031921Z", "close_reason": "Completed hard-parts truth table reconciliation across README/FEATURE_PARITY/support_matrix/reality_report with new drift guard script, harness test, and CI wiring.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["critique", "docs", "drift", "hard-parts", "verification"], "comments": [{"id": 1063, "issue_id": "bd-8sho", "author": "Dicklesworthstone", "text": "RedMaple claiming bd-8sho and starting hard-parts truth reconciliation across README/FEATURE_PARITY/support_matrix/reality_report.\n\nPlanned deliverables:\n1)  artifact with per-subsystem implemented vs deferred rationale.\n2)  drift guard script.\n3)  guard test.\n4) doc updates in  and  to include explicit hard-parts truth lines tied to the artifact.", "created_at": "2026-02-13T08:48:02Z"}, {"id": 1064, "issue_id": "bd-8sho", "author": "Dicklesworthstone", "text": "Progress update (RedMaple): completed hard-parts docs/parity/support/reality truth reconciliation with drift guard wiring.\n\nImplemented:\n- New canonical artifact: tests/conformance/hard_parts_truth_table.v1.json\n  - per-subsystem status + implemented/deferred scope rationale for startup/threading/resolver/nss/locale/iconv\n  - support_matrix expectations per subsystem\n  - reality_report snapshot binding + contradiction summary\n- New drift guard: scripts/check_hard_parts_truth.sh\n  - validates artifact schema, README + FEATURE_PARITY hard-parts truth lines, support_matrix expectations, and reality_report snapshot alignment\n  - emits structured JSON event with trace_id/mode/api_family/outcome/artifact refs\n- New harness test: crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/hard_parts_truth_test.rs\n  - artifact schema validation\n  - guard script execution gate\n- Docs reconciled:\n  - README.md hard-parts truth section added\n  - FEATURE_PARITY.md hard-parts truth section added\n- CI wiring:\n  - scripts/ci.sh now runs scripts/check_hard_parts_truth.sh\n\nVerification executed (PASS):\n- scripts/check_hard_parts_truth.sh\n- CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/cargo-target-codex-harness cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test hard_parts_truth_test\n- CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/cargo-target-codex-harness cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test parity_report_drift_test\n\nNote:\n- /tmp is full on this host; verification used CARGO_TARGET_DIR under /data/tmp for deterministic local runs.", "created_at": "2026-02-13T08:52:17Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-8zsp","title":"Smoke-fuzz proptests for inet IP parsers","description":"parse_ipv4, parse_ipv6, inet_pton, inet_aton in frankenlibc_core::inet are classic security-sensitive parsers. Add Archetype-1 smoke-fuzz proptests: (a) never-panics on arbitrary bytes, (b) output bounded, (c) format-roundtrip for inputs that parse successfully (parse(format(x)) == x). Scope: crates/frankenlibc-core/src/inet/mod.rs #[cfg(test)] module.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T20:29:16.512627925Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T20:31:46.178725431Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T20:31:46.176686404Z","close_reason":"Added 8 inet smoke-fuzz proptests in commit 0cf34de: parse_ipv4 (never-panics, structured-alphabet, round-trip), parse_ipv6 (never-panics, structured-alphabet, round-trip), inet_pton (af-cube), inet_aton (legacy). ~4100 case evaluations per run, no panics found on first sweep. Covers historical CVE surface for dotted-quad arithmetic and IPv6 '::' expansion.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-95tq","title":"resolv: gai_cancel and gai_suspend empty requests should return EAI_ALLDONE","description":"Concrete regression: host glibc returns EAI_ALLDONE with errno unchanged for synchronous-degenerate async DNS calls like gai_cancel(NULL), gai_cancel(&zeroed_gaicb), gai_suspend(NULL, 0, timeout), and gai_suspend([NULL], 1, timeout). FrankenLibC currently returns EAI_SYSTEM with ENOSYS. Restore host-parity degenerate semantics in unistd_abi.rs and lock them with focused ABI tests.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T06:14:54.765525235Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T06:16:32.621315051Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-95zf","title":"AI_ADDRCONFIG review: null-node IPv6-only and dual-stack coverage","description":"Review of cod's bd-4qqu fix (commit 8996ad1 hoisting AI_ADDRCONFIG filter out of Some(text) branch) found 2 coverage gaps in the null-node test set: (1) IPv6-only interface state — the null-node IPv4 filter case is tested but the symmetric IPv6-only case is not, so an asymmetric filter bug would escape. (2) dual-stack interface state with null-node — no test verifies both AF_INET and AF_INET6 nodes are preserved when both families have non-loopback routes. Scope: crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/resolv_abi_test.rs additions only, no code changes.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T19:37:35.826818907Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T19:38:54.778398472Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T19:38:54.776390943Z","close_reason":"Added 2 complementary null-node AI_ADDRCONFIG tests in commit b935235: IPv6-only filter symmetry + dual-stack preservation. Suite now covers all 4 quadrants of the {IPv4,IPv6} x {filter,keep} matrix for null-node under AI_ADDRCONFIG.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-96nv","title":"Conformance: direct execute string_strtok fixture cases","description":"Add direct frankenlibc_fixture_exec coverage for string_strtok.json cases in the harness alongside existing isolated harness coverage.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SilverHarbor","created_at":"2026-04-18T07:48:45.060551994Z","created_by":"SilverHarbor","updated_at":"2026-04-18T07:54:40.353901880Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T07:54:40.352014970Z","close_reason":"Added direct execute_fixture_case coverage for string_strtok fixtures and verified with rch test/check/clippy.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","string","testing"]}
 {"id":"bd-9chy.10","title":"stdio: export _IO_2_1_stdin_/stdout_/stderr_ aliases bound to NativeFile storage","description":"## Background

Plan v4 §3 (final paragraph). Some statically-linked-against-libc binaries resolve _IO_2_1_stdin_, _IO_2_1_stdout_, _IO_2_1_stderr_ directly via dlsym or link-time resolution, bypassing the public stdin/stdout/stderr aliases. Without exporting these names from FrankenLibC, the binary sees two distinct streams (one native FrankenLibC, one host glibc) and the program misbehaves subtly: partial flushes, lost output, double-close.

## Depends on

bd-9chy.1 (NativeFile layout)

## What to do

1. Add the three symbol names to crates/frankenlibc-abi/version_scripts/libc.map under the GLIBC_2.2.5 version tag.
2. In stdio_abi.rs (or io_internal_abi.rs), declare the underscored names as #[no_mangle] static aliases pointing to the *same* NativeFile storage as the public stdin/stdout/stderr globals. Do NOT allocate a second set of NativeFile objects — they must be the same object so a flush via stdin reaches the same buffer as a flush via _IO_2_1_stdin_.
3. Verify the alias by building the .so and inspecting nm: nm -D target/release/libfrankenlibc_abi.so | grep _IO_2_1 must show the three symbols at the same address as stdin/stdout/stderr.
4. Add an integration test: build a small C program that takes both stdin and _IO_2_1_stdin_, asserts they are equal, and runs it under LD_PRELOAD.

## Done

- libc.map exports the three aliases
- nm verification passes
- Integration test passes
- The aliases survive a release build with strip+lto","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:28:41.061649453Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T00:24:31.706206591Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["foundation","stdio","version-script"]}
@@ -534,6 +536,7 @@
 {"id": "bd-blg", "title": "bd-c1x subtask: Condvar semantics spec (spurious wakeups, timeout clocks, errno rules)", "description": "Background:
- Condition variables are subtle (spurious wakeups, timeout clocks, predicate loops) and require precise semantics before implementation.

Goal:
- Produce clean-room condvar semantics spec including clock handling and spurious-wakeup contract.

Deliverables:
1) wait/signal/broadcast/timedwait semantics table.
2) CLOCK_REALTIME vs CLOCK_MONOTONIC timeout conversion rules.
3) Spurious wakeup policy and required caller predicate-loop guidance.
4) Error mapping and invalid-usage handling.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Spec is implementation-ready and self-contained.
- Deferred/unsupported semantics explicitly documented.

Verification & Logging:
- Unit tests for timeout conversion helpers and semantic table consistency.
- Structured logs for spec-check tools and generated matrices.", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "task", "assignee": "PurpleHawk", "created_at": "2026-02-12T15:00:51.435304263Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-02-13T17:54:28.070333386Z", "closed_at": "2026-02-13T17:54:28.070263545Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["critique", "futex", "pthread", "spec", "testing"], "comments": [{"id": 1075, "issue_id": "bd-blg", "author": "PurpleHawk", "text": "Completed condvar semantics spec (bd-blg). Deliverables:

1) cond_contract.md: Full transition table for init/destroy/wait/timedwait/signal/broadcast with state model (Uninitialized/Idle/Waiting/Destroyed).

2) cond.rs expanded with executable clean-room contract: CondvarContractState enum, CondvarContractOp enum, CondvarContractOutcome struct, condvar_contract_transition() const fn, CondvarAttributeContract with process_shared deferred, valid_timespec_nsec validator, futex_condvar_contention_note(), spurious_wakeup_policy().

3) 41 unit tests pass covering: all state x operation transitions, attribute support/deferral, timespec validation, lifecycle scenarios, signal/broadcast semantics, and policy documentation.

4) Conformance fixture: tests/conformance/fixtures/pthread_cond.json with 24 test cases covering all 6 operations, clock handling, null pointer checks, timeout semantics, mutex mismatch, spurious wakeup pattern, and lifecycle.

5) Spec explicitly addresses: CLOCK_REALTIME/MONOTONIC timeout conversion, spurious wakeup predicate-loop requirement, mutex association invariant (all concurrent waiters same mutex), futex requeue optimization for broadcast, and deferred features (process-shared).

Verification: cargo test -p frankenlibc-core -- pthread::cond (41 passed, 0 failed). cargo check --all-targets clean.", "created_at": "2026-02-13T17:54:23Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-bpe6","title":"Performance: stdio calls 4500x slower due to membrane overhead","notes":"puts() baseline: 31ns/call, with LD_PRELOAD: 138us/call (4500x slower). Root cause: each stdio call goes through runtime_policy::decide(), healing checks, scan_c_str_len(), and observe(). This causes smoke test performance failures (>2x threshold). Need fast-path bypass for hot stdio functions or optimized policy decision path.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"SilverHarbor","created_at":"2026-04-18T00:45:22.050436442Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T01:18:53.265027499Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T01:18:53.262467997Z","close_reason":"Implemented strict/default stdio write fast path; rch cargo check -p frankenlibc-abi --tests passed; focused assertions pass, with pre-existing stdio_abi_test teardown SIGSEGV tracked separately.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":1415,"issue_id":"bd-bpe6","author":"SilverHarbor","text":"Implemented strict/default fast path for hot stdio writes in stdio_abi.rs (fputc/fputs/fwrite/puts) so they bypass runtime_policy::decide/observe outside hardened mode while preserving hardened policy behavior. Formatted and diff-check clean; holding focused rch test until active peer builds clear.","created_at":"2026-04-18T01:11:59Z"},{"id":1416,"issue_id":"bd-bpe6","author":"SilverHarbor","text":"Verification: rch cargo check -p frankenlibc-abi --tests passed. Focused test strict_hot_stdio_writes_skip_runtime_policy_gate reported all assertions ok, but stdio_abi_test binary still exits SIGSEGV after teardown; existing puts_calls_without_crash and fputc_fgetc_and_ungetc_behave_consistently show the same ok-then-SIGSEGV pattern, so this is the pre-existing stdio ABI harness teardown crash rather than this fast-path assertion failing.","created_at":"2026-04-18T01:18:27Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-bpx9","title":"syscall: implement raw clone3","description":"Add a typed raw Linux clone3 syscall veneer in frankenlibc-core, plus focused syscall veneer tests covering invalid struct size and null-pointer error paths. This extends the native syscall layer for future pthread/process work under the glibc-removal epic.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-19T23:45:18.494657402Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T23:49:43.437157548Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T23:49:43.435160269Z","close_reason":"Added typed raw clone3 veneer in frankenlibc-core with focused syscall veneer coverage and passed rch fmt/check/clippy/test gates.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-bw0h","title":"resolv: add host-parity ABI probes for synchronous-only gai_* wrappers","description":"Concrete coverage gap: unistd_abi_test.rs currently hard-codes ENOSYS expectations for the getaddrinfo_a/gai_* wrapper family and does not fence the observed host-glibc degenerate semantics for null, empty, and zeroed-request inputs. Add explicit host-parity ABI tests so future stub regressions are caught immediately.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T06:14:54.947647155Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T06:14:54.947647155Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Delivered alongside design (bd-15q). EWMA-smoothed 5-signal input, 5-arm Euler-weighted scoring, O(1) integer-only. Clippy clean.", "created_at": "2026-02-10T17:40:17Z"}]}
 {"id": "bd-by8c", "title": "bd-3bg subtask: Runtime iconv dispatch integration with explicit fallback/error policy", "description": "Background:
- Runtime codec selection must remain deterministic and safe even with partial codec coverage.

Goal:
- Integrate generated codec tables into runtime iconv path with explicit fallback and error policy.

Deliverables:
1) Runtime lookup/dispatch integration.
2) Fallback behavior for unsupported codecs.
3) Mode-aware safety handling and telemetry.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Supported conversions succeed deterministically.
- Unsupported conversions fail according to explicit contract.

Verification & Logging:
- Unit tests for supported/unsupported conversion paths.
- Structured logs with trace_id, codec_pair, dispatch_path, outcome, errno.", "acceptance_criteria": "## Acceptance Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.", "notes": "### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "task", "assignee": "ScarletBasin", "created_at": "2026-02-12T15:01:54.486299908Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-13T23:53:30.710296662Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-13T23:53:30.710097235Z", "close_reason": "done", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["critique", "iconv", "implementation", "testing", "verification"], "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "bd-by8c", "depends_on_id": "bd-13ya", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:32Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-by8c", "depends_on_id": "bd-7cba", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:32Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}]}
 {"id": "bd-byd9", "title": "EPIC: Flat Combining for Contended State [Score 6.0, section 14.2]", "description": "Implement flat combining for sequentially batching operations under contention. When multiple threads contend on shared state (e.g., global allocation statistics, arena rebalancing counters, evidence ledger writes), flat combining designates one thread as the 'combiner' that executes all pending operations sequentially, eliminating lock contention. How it works: (1) Each thread publishes its pending operation to a per-thread slot in a shared publication list. (2) One thread acquires the combiner lock. (3) The combiner scans all publication slots, collects pending operations, and executes them all sequentially. (4) The combiner writes results back to each thread's slot. (5) Other threads spin-wait on their slot until the result appears. Benefits: (a) Sequential execution eliminates all synchronization within the combined batch. (b) Cache locality — the combiner's working set stays hot. (c) Throughput scales better than lock-based approaches under high contention because the combiner amortizes lock acquisition cost across N operations. Target applications in FrankenLibC: global allocation statistics (malloc_stats/mallinfo), arena rebalancing decisions, evidence ledger batch writes, and TSM validation pipeline under burst load.

## Success Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.", "design": "**Alien CS Reference:** Section 14.2 of graveyard (Score 6.0). Flat combining from Hendler, Hendler & Shavit (2010 SPAA). Publication list pattern for sequential batching under contention.

**Rust Implementation Guidance:**
- FlatCombiner<T, Op, R> generic implementation in src/concurrency/flat_combining.rs.
- First application: global allocation statistics (AllocStats).
- Future applications: arena rebalancing, evidence ledger batch writes, TSM pipeline burst handling.", "acceptance_criteria": "## Success Criteria
1. FlatCombiner generic implemented and applied to AllocStats.
2. Throughput >10x mutex at 32 threads for stat-heavy workloads.
3. No lost operations under any contention level.
4. Benchmark comparing flat combining vs mutex, RwLock, atomic counters.
5. Crossover point documented (thread count where flat combining wins).

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.", "notes": "**Logging Requirements:**
- Combiner activity at trace level. Benchmark results as JSON artifacts.

### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "epic", "created_at": "2026-02-13T09:23:50.144746144Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-07T23:04:41.325595364Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-07T23:04:41.325362824Z", "close_reason": "Delivered or tracked as future work.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "comments": [{"id": 1077, "issue_id": "bd-byd9", "author": "Dicklesworthstone", "text": "Alien+Extreme uplift pass (2026-02-13): this epic is now covered by the full open-backlog EV matrix at artifacts/planning/open_beads_alien_uplift.v1.json (summary markdown: artifacts/planning/open_beads_alien_uplift.v1.md).

Execution contract applied:
- One-lever optimization waves only (profile -> prove -> implement -> verify).
- Budgeted mode + deterministic fallback trigger required per child bead.
- Graveyard route mappings and baseline comparators are pre-attached in matrix rows.
- Promotion gate: EV >= 2.0 and no cycle introduction in bv --robot-insights.", "created_at": "2026-02-13T22:25:08Z"}]}

 {"id": "bd-wuh", "title": "EPIC: RaptorQ-inspired runtime resilience (erasure-coded evidence + metadata self-healing)", "description": "Goal: use RaptorQ patterns accretively inside libc runtime, without adding /dp/asupersync as a runtime dependency.

Core idea (pattern, not copy-paste):
- Systematic fast path: store source symbols (raw evidence) cheaply.
- Repair path: generate deterministic repair symbols on a cadence (epoch boundary), not per call.
- Decode + proof: decoding and explainability happen in diagnostics/harness, not on the strict hot path.

Candidate uses:
1) Erasure-coded evidence ring buffer for runtime_math decisions/heals/anomalies.
2) Self-healing for small critical metadata capsules (headers/roots), not entire large tables.
3) Cross-shard summary reconstruction to support cohomology/consistency monitors under partial loss.

Success criteria:
- Strict fast path unchanged; hardened adds bounded overhead only when enabled.
- Deterministic encoding parameters + evidence-ledger tuning of redundancy.", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "feature", "created_at": "2026-02-09T21:30:15.573551832Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-02-11T03:03:13.254498204Z", "closed_at": "2026-02-11T03:03:13.145127019Z", "close_reason": "done", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "bd-wuh", "depends_on_id": "bd-284", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:34Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-wuh", "depends_on_id": "bd-2xr", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:34Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-wuh", "depends_on_id": "bd-3a9", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:34Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-wuh", "depends_on_id": "bd-3ku", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:34Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-wuh", "depends_on_id": "bd-pc4", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:34Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}], "comments": [{"id": 1215, "issue_id": "bd-wuh", "author": "Dicklesworthstone", "text": "## Epic Notes: RaptorQ-Inspired Resilience Inside libc Runtime

### North Star
Turn fragile “best effort” telemetry into **recoverable, explainable evidence** under partial loss, while keeping libc hot paths fast.

RaptorQ is the reference pattern because it gives us:
- systematic fast path (zero overhead when nothing is lost)
- deterministic repair generation (no coordination required)
- recovery from erasures (dropped/missing records) with small overhead
- explainability via decode proofs (in tooling)

### Non-Negotiable Constraints
- No `/dp/asupersync` in the runtime dependency graph.
- Strict mode hot path stays “boring”: constant-time, no allocations, no locks.
- Hardened mode overhead is bounded and measured (microbench + regression gates).

### Concrete libc Use Cases (Scope)
1. **Erasure-coded evidence ring**
   - Evidence includes: runtime_math decision + key context + membrane outcome + heal action + stage exits.
   - Goal: if we drop N% of events or overwrite parts of the ring, tooling can still reconstruct a consistent prefix / windows.
2. **Self-healing critical metadata capsules**
   - Small, high-value state snapshots (kernel snapshot schema, policy tables hash, config epoch) should survive partial corruption.
3. **Cross-shard reconstruction for consistency monitors**
   - Cohomology/consistency monitors can treat “missing overlap witnesses” as erasures and reconstruct summaries.

### Runtime Design Principles (Extreme Optimization)
- Evidence recording per call:
  - `seq = atomic_fetch_add(Relaxed)`
  - store a fixed-size record into a per-thread ring slot
  - no heap alloc
  - no syscalls
  - no GF(256) operations
- Repair symbol generation:
  - done on cadence: every `K_source` events, per-thread or global epoch
  - uses deterministic seed; work amortized
  - may run in a background worker thread only in hardened mode
- Export:
  - tooling/harness reads the ring (or a frozen snapshot) and performs decode + proof.

### Reliability Model
We model losses as:
- erasures: records not present (ring overwrite, sampling drop, partial capture)
- corruption: payload hash mismatch (bit flips, races)

We treat corruption as “erase and try to repair” (don’t let bad bytes poison decode).

### Adaptive Redundancy (Control)
- Observe: loss rate (erasures) and corruption rate in exported traces.
- Decide: raise redundancy when anytime-valid evidence says we’re violating durability/observability budgets.
- Act: increase `R` (repair symbols) in future epochs; optionally retro-harden recent epochs.

This is implemented via `bd-284` and wired to runtime_math e-process + evidence ledger.

### Deliverables / Done Means
- Encoding path exists with strict-mode overhead indistinguishable from baseline.
- Offline decode exists and verifies record integrity + hash-chain.
- Loss/corruption simulation tests demonstrate reconstruction up to documented budgets.
- Evidence reports are explainable: when repair happens, tooling emits a DecodeProof.", "created_at": "2026-02-09T21:50:51Z"}, {"id": 1216, "issue_id": "bd-wuh", "author": "Dicklesworthstone", "text": "EPIC COMPLETE. All 5 blockers resolved: bd-3a9 (architecture), bd-pc4 (offline decoder), bd-2xr (loss simulation), bd-3ku (evidence integration), bd-284 (adaptive tuner). Evidence ring buffer integrated, adaptive redundancy tuner with e-process + audit ledger, 69 modules, all quality gates passing.", "created_at": "2026-02-11T03:03:13Z"}]}
